title: viola hirta subsp. calcarea
intro: Viola hirta subsp. calcarea is a subspecies of the hairy violet plant, a perennial herb native to Europe, with a distinctive hairy appearance and white flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, attributed to its rich content of flavonoids and glycosides. The medicinal properties of Viola hirta subsp. calcarea are believed to provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antiseptic effects. Herbal preparations of the plant can be found in the form of infusions, tinctures, and ointments, often used to treat minor skin irritations, bruises, and other superficial injuries.
